q:
The farmer walking next to his cows is …

A. ... a driver.
B. ... a pedestrian.
e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
A farmer walking next to his cows is a driver.
A driver is someone accompanying or guarding draft animals, farm animals, horses on the public road.
clean_q:
The farmer walking next to his cows is … 
A. ... a driver.
B. ... a pedestrian.
clean_e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
A farmer walking next to his cows is a driver.
A driver is someone accompanying or guarding draft animals, farm animals, horses on the public road.

q: A car you can drive with a driving licence B, is a car with a M.A.W. of maximum (…) kg.
e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
Vehicles with a M.A.W. of maximum 3.5 ton.
Car – a car for double use – minibus – light truck.

Car – a car for double use – minibus – light truck.

q:

A. ... a driver.
B. ... a pedestrian.
e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
A driver is someone accompanying or guarding draft animals, farm animals, horses on the public road.

q:
How do you react when you see a scared horse on the way?

A. Simply continue driving.
B. Delay.
C. Stopping.
e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
Every driver should immediately slow down when approaching pulling animals, welding, driving or livestock on public roads. He had to stop if these animals show signs of fear.

q:
Dad is walking in the street with his son who is sitting on a pony.
Dad is …

A. A pedestrian.
B. A driver.
e:
LESSON 7
A rider or a person who leads a horse is not a pedestrian, but a driver under the traffic rules.
clean_q:
Dad is walking in the street with his son who is sitting on a pony.
Dad is …
A. A pedestrian.
B. A driver.
clean_e:
LESSON 7
A rider or a person who leads a horse is not a pedestrian, but a driver under the traffic rules.

q: Are you allowed to drive a light truck with a temporary licence B?
e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
This is a light truck.
All vehicles with a M.A.W. of maximum 3.5 ton.
Car – a car for double use – minibus – light truck.

q:
This man is pushing a broken down motorcycle. What is the most complete and correct answer?

A. He must wear a helmet.
B. He must wear a helmet and walk on the road.
C. He must wear a helmet, walk on the road and have a driving licence.
e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
It is a driver of a motorcycle.
He needs: a driving licence A, a helmet and has to push the motorcycle on the road.
clean_q:
This man is pushing a broken down motorcycle. What is the most complete and correct answer? 
A. He must wear a helmet.
B. He must wear a helmet and walk on the road. 
C. He must wear a helmet, walk on the road and have a driving licence.
clean_e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
It is a driver of a motorcycle.
He needs: a driving licence A, a helmet and has to push the motorcycle on the road.

q:
A shepherd walking on the road with his sheep is...

A. A pedestrian.
B. A driver.
e:
LESSON 8
In the road code, he is considered a driver.
clean_q:
A shepherd walking on the road with his sheep is...
A. A pedestrian.
B. A driver.
clean_e:
LESSON 8
In the road code, he is considered a driver.

q:
A driver who gets out of the car and is now pushing the car …

A. ... remains a driver.
B. ... becomes a pedestrian.
e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
Who pushes a car or motorcycle, remains a driver.
Who pushes a moped is according to the traffic regulations a pedestrian.

clean_e:
LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.

Who pushes a moped is according to the traffic regulations a pedestrian.

LESSON 8 – ‘The drivers’.
